title: tetragonia halimoides
intro: Tetragonia halimoides, also known as New Zealand spinach or kōkihi, is a perennial succulent plant native to New Zealand and Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Tetragonia halimoides include its ability to treat constipation, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, Tetragonia halimoides is often used as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be consumed raw in salads or as a cooked vegetable.
